summary: Implemented Save Envelope v1 as an explicit DTO/codec package with schema, checksum, size/depth, semantic validation hooks, M1 golden fixture, sim-core request-save/load-save integration, Node/worker save-load canary, and 30-node M1 performance baseline.

tests_run.result: 2 test files passed; 5 tests passed. Covers golden round-trip, hash preservation, corrupt checksum, unknown schema, bad reference, truncated payload, contentManifestHash mismatch, and Node/Worker save-load contract.
6 test files passed; 29 tests passed. Existing warning only: MODULE_TYPELESS_PACKAGE_JSON for tests/fixtures/deterministic-rng-worker.ts.
Node/worker hello hash 61e0ba1c matched; command/query hash d275faa4 matched; save/load hash 28074edd matched; save/load byte length 10920.
Architecture boundaries passed; self-test fixture produced 15 expected violations.

Root test command passed: architecture check, sim determinism, 13 root Vitest files/50 tests, and web Vitest 1 file/3 tests. Existing MODULE_TYPELESS_PACKAGE_JSON warning appeared for deterministic-rng-worker.ts.

SIM-005 baseline on m1.abstract-graph-30: 3650 days, finalDay 3650, finalRevision 3650, throughput 13068 days/sec, p50 59700ns, p95 135300ns, p99 224300ns, saveByteLength 9980, save 2349700ns, load 3377400ns.
All matched files use Prettier code style.
Recursive workspace typecheck passed, including @monsoon/save-format and @monsoon/sim-core.

decisions: Added @monsoon/save-format as a pure TypeScript DTO/codec package with no platform IO, no compression, no database, and no new external production dependency.

Save Envelope v1 uses explicit magic, schemaVersion, build metadata, contentManifestHash, scenarioId, seed, currentDay, checksumAlgorithm, checksum, authoritative WorldState v0 snapshot DTO, scheduler DTO, RNG compatibility hook, and bounded command/event tails.
Checksum is FNV-1a over canonical save body JSON. Header contentManifestHash/scenario mismatches remain structured load rejections instead of being hidden by checksum failures.

Load rejects malformed JSON, size/depth violations, unsupported versions, checksum mismatch, content/scenario mismatch, schema errors, and sim-core semantic invariant failures without mutating the caller's current runtime.
Added synthetic m1.abstract-graph-30 sim-core fixture using manifest hash 4a438525 and kept the pre-existing minimal-m1 canary intact.
Golden fixture checksum is e6588316 and initial M1 graph state hash is f336958d; tests/fixtures/save-format/README.md records the intentional update workflow.
risks: R3 save/schema change requires qa_reviewer scrutiny.
Save event tail validation is intentionally narrow for M1: it preserves known current DomainEvent DTOs but does not introduce full event sourcing or migration beyond v1 rejection scaffolding.
Sim-core now depends on the pure save-format package for request-save/load-save helpers; architecture:check passes, but QA should verify this package boundary is acceptable under docs/04.
requested_action: Please review SIM-005 for save envelope schema correctness, rejection safety/no-mutation behavior, golden fixture workflow, Node/Worker shared codec coverage, and R3 compatibility risk.
